title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Capstone Defense · Stakeholder Container Mission
summary: An engineering and security review board. The board needs a two-platform image release with immutable identity, tested Compose operation, least privilege, SBOM, provenance, vulnerability policy, recovery rehearsal, upgrade plan, and rollback defense. Planned artifact: Deliver Secure Multi-Platform Production Release Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and BuildKit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance.
Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.
Generate, attach, inspect, export, validate, and interpret SPDX SBOM attestations including build-stage and final-image coverage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Docker Reproducible Build Evidence
steps.instruction:
project case — A project board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Docker Reproducible Build Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and buildkit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Docker Capstone Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-capstone-production-defense-11cmbe". Tailor its production-defense.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Docker Capstone Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Capstone Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Docker Capstone Production Defense, keep Docker Capstone Production Defense connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-capstone-production-defense-11cmbe
  - competency: docker-capstone-production-defense
    artifact: production-defense.yaml
    decision: capstone production defense
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: multi-tenant build farm using an isolated BuildKit driver
    fault-injection: fill the writable layer before graceful termination
    retained-proof: shutdown duration exit state and durable-work reconciliation
    case-key: 11cmbe
    change: |
      release: digest-pinned
      rollout: health-and-observation-gated
      rollback: rehearsed-previous-digest
      backup-restore: timed-and-proven
      upgrade: compatibility-canary
      capacity: measured-limits
      handoff: accessible-runbook
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject latest-tag rollout, untested rollback, backup without restore, blind daemon upgrade, or a container boundary claimed as orchestrator durability.
    repair: Defend the operating model with immutable identity, canary signals, failure drills, recovery objectives, capacity evidence, ownership, and residual risks.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
